originalTitle: Peak Design’s modular Field Bracket has a finder tag built-in
summary: I am a very clumsy man. So clumsy, that I have AirTags hanging off practically every camera I own. Have I left my camera in an Uber? Yes. Have I left it on an airplane? Also yes. For me, finder tags are essential for my cameras. The problem is, strapping a finder tag to a […]
content: I am a very clumsy man. So clumsy, that I have AirTags hanging off practically every camera I own. Have I left my camera in an Uber? Yes. Have I left it on an airplane? Also yes. For me, finder tags are essential for my cameras. The problem is, strapping a finder tag to a camera is always cumbersome. I currently have them hanging off my camera straps like a phone charm circa 2001. They’re ugly, and very much get in the way when I’m trying to focus on shooting. But Peak Design thinks it has a solution in its new Field Bracket system, which it launched today on Kickstarter, starting at $150 for early birds. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Peak-Design-Lifestyle-Field-Bracket.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] The Peak Design Field Bracket is part L-Bracket, part hand grip, part cheese plate. It’s made for photographers and videographers who want to quickly switch between landscape and portrait orientation on their tripod, or sometimes want to mount a light or mic to their camera when there isn’t a dedicated place to do so. But possibly the most enticing thing, to me at least, is that the bracket comes with a slot for a dedicated Finder Tag. Peak’s Beacon Finder Tag is a Peak Design-branded finder that works with Apple Find My and Google Find Hub. That means it’ll work with both Apple and Android phones, so you don’t have to worry about losing access to your camera finder if you switch ecosystems. The tag uses a standard CR2025 battery, and Peak says it’s rated to last up to 6 months. It’s also rated for IP67 water and dust resistance, and it fits flush inside the base plate of the L-bracket. The Beacon Finder Tag is just one of the accessories Peak Design is selling alongside the Field Bracket. The base product you’ll be buying is in fact an L-bracket, but once that’s on your camera, you’ll have access to other add-on accessories like a hand grip, finger grip, and cheese plate. Peak says the Field Bracket will work with most ARCA-compatible tripods, and won’t get in the way if you use it with its own capture clip or camera straps. In fact, the bracket itself has dedicated slots to attach your Peak Design camera strap, so you won’t have to sacrifice the strap you own to use the bracket. Peak calls the vertical attachment to the L-bracket the Field Latch, and it uses the company’s new locking system to quickly attach and detach it without any tools. This is the same place you attach the optional cheese plate, which pulls out in adjustable stops in case you need to access ports or run cables from the camera. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Peak-Design-Field-Bracket-2.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] Of course, you’ll have to choose the exact bracket you want for the camera you own, considering it’s molded specifically to that body. At launch, most cameras supported are the newest, or near-newest models of each camera line. You can see a compatibility list here. And if you want to see your camera supported, Peak has a dedicated feedback page to submit the camera you own. The bracket itself will MSRP at $149.95, and if you want a bundle of all the accessories currently offered, you can get everything for $269.80. As usual, though, Peak Design is offering a Kickstarter discount, which puts the bracket at $125 and the bundle at $199, while supplies last at least.
chineseTitle: Peak Design 的模块化现场支架内置有取景器标签
chineseSummary: 我是一个非常笨拙的人。太笨拙了，我几乎把 AirTags 挂在我拥有的每台相机上。我是否把相机落在了 Uber 车上？是的。我把它落在飞机上了吗？也是的。对我来说，取景器标签对于我的相机来说是必不可少的。问题是，将查找器标签绑在 [...]
chineseContent: 我是一个非常笨拙的人。太笨拙了，我几乎把 AirTags 挂在我拥有的每台相机上。我是否把相机落在了 Uber 车上？是的。我把它落在飞机上了吗？也是的。对我来说，取景器标签对于我的相机来说是必不可少的。问题是，将取景器标签绑在相机上总是很麻烦。目前，我把它们挂在我的相机带上，就像 2001 年左右的手机吊饰一样。它们很丑，而且当我试图专注于拍摄时，它们非常妨碍我。但 Peak Design 认为其新的 Field Bracket 系统有解决方案，该系统今天在 Kickstarter 上推出，早鸟价为 150 美元。 [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Peak-Design-Lifestyle-Field-Bracket.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] Peak Design Field 支架部分是 L 型支架，部分是手柄，部分是奶酪盘。它专为想要在三脚架上快速切换横向和纵向方向的摄影师和摄像师而设计，或者有时想要在没有专用位置的情况下在相机上安装灯或麦克风。但至少对我来说，最吸引人的可能是支架带有一个用于专用 Finder 标签的插槽。 Peak 的 Beacon Finder Tag 是一款 Peak Design 品牌的查找器，可与 Apple Find My 和 Google Find Hub 配合使用。这意味着它适用于 Apple 和 Android 手机，因此您不必担心在切换生态系统时会失去对相机取景器的访问权限。该标签使用标准 CR2025 电池，Peak 表示其额定续航时间长达 6 个月。它还具有 IP67 防水防尘等级，并且齐平安装在 L 型支架的底板内。信标查找器标签只是 Peak Design 与现场支架一起销售的配件之一。您将购买的基础产品实际上是
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
